[2026-04-04 22:52-23:00] Explored how nanobot, Claude Code, and Mahavishnu integrate. Key findings:
1. Nanobot (persistent Slack assistant, v0.1.4.post6) and Claude Code (interactive CLI coding agent) serve different but complementary roles.
2. Examined nanobot's MCP server support — nanobot uses MCPServerConfig schema supporting stdio/sse/streamableHttp transports.
3. Extracted all MCP server configs from ~/.claude.json (Claude Code) to replicate in nanobot config.
4. Updated nanobot config at /Users/les/.nanobot/config.json with MCP servers including mahavishnu, crackerjack, session-buddy, akosha, dhara, codex, grafana, raindropio, n8n, neo4j, homebrew, web-search-prime, web-reader, macos_automator, peekaboo, gitlab.
5. Discovered `claude mcp serve` command — Claude Code can itself run as an MCP server.
6. Nanobot uses "streamableHttp" type for HTTP MCP endpoints (not "http" like Claude Code config format).
7. Codex MCP server at /Users/les/.claude/scripts/codex-mcp-server.sh wraps /usr/local/bin/codex mcp-server.
8. All Bodai ecosystem ports (8676, 8678, 8680, 8682, 8683, 3034, 3035, 3044, 3045) were DOWN at time of check.

Configured 16 MCP servers in nanobot config (~/.nanobot/config.json under tools.mcpServers). Servers added: mahavishnu, session-buddy, crackerjack, akosha, dhara, codex, homebrew, n8n, neo4j, grafana, raindropio, web-search-prime, web-reader, gitlab, macos-automator, peekaboo. Intentionally excluded: css, chart-antv, excalidraw, mermaid, penpot, opera-cloud, synxis-crs, synxis-pms, mailgun, unifi, porkbun-dns, porkbun-domain, postman, zai-mcp-server, pycharm (reasons: visualization tools not useful for text chat agent, domain-specific hospitality, ops tools, duplicate of existing Zhipu APIs, IDE-specific). Confirmed `claude mcp serve` is stdio-based MCP server (speaks over stdin/stdout). Noted recursive loop risk if nesting Claude Code inside nanobot. Both nanobot and Claude Code share workspace but run independently.

Analyzed Session-Buddy MCP server compatibility with Nanobot. Deep-dived into session-buddy source code (server_optimized.py, adapters/lifecycle.py, mcp/event_models.py) and nanobot MCP client (nanobot/agent/tools/mcp.py, agent/loop.py). Key findings: Session-Buddy is mostly compatible with Nanobot today via MCP tools (checkpoint, store_reflection, quick_search, track_session_start/end all work). The FastMCP lifespan hook auto-starts/ends sessions for Claude Code but doesn't fire for Nanobot (nanobot is a long-running daemon, connects once). Session-Buddy has generic track_session_start/end tools with Pydantic event models accepting any component_name. Has Claude-specific _setup_claude_directories() but harmless. Recommended nanobot-side integration: call track_session_start on gateway startup, track_session_end on shutdown, pre_compact_sync after memory consolidation, and periodic auto-checkpoints (~50-100 lines). Session-Buddy v0.14.7 confirmed.

Decided to implement Session-Buddy lifecycle integration for nanobot as a nanobot skill (SKILL.md) rather than modifying nanobot's Python source code. Drafted comprehensive Claude Code prompt to create skill at /Users/les/Projects/mahavishnu/skills/session-buddy/SKILL.md. Nanobot installed as package only (no local source repo) — found at two locations: .venv and uv tools. Skill approach chosen because it requires zero code changes to either project. Skill will handle: auto session start (per day), manual session end, manual checkpoints, pre-compaction sync, and periodic auto-checkpoint reminders.

Created the Session-Buddy + Nanobot integration skill at /Users/les/Projects/mahavishnu/skills/session-buddy/SKILL.md (77 lines). Skill provides: auto session start/end tracking via MCP tools, manual checkpoint, pre-compact sync, and 2-hour auto-checkpoint reminder using cron. Skill references exec tool for runtime values (uuid, pid, hostname). State persisted in MEMORY.md under Session-Buddy State section. Status changed from "awaiting execution" to "created". Next step: nanobot needs to pick up the new skill (auto-discovery from workspace skills/ dir or restart).

Successfully edited /Users/les/Projects/mahavishnu/memory/MEMORY.md to add a `## Session-Buddy State` section. This gives the session-buddy Nanobot skill a place to track the active session ID, last checkpoint, and session start time. The skill creation is now complete (SKILL.md at /Users/les/Projects/mahavishnu/skills/session-buddy/SKILL.md + MEMORY.md state section). Next step: nanobot restart to pick up the new skill.

Investigated turning nanobot into a macOS LaunchAgent service. Found all Bodai ecosystem MCP servers (29 plists) already have LaunchAgents in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ following a consistent pattern. Also found com.mcp.supervisor.plist exists. Nanobot currently runs manually from terminal. Three options assessed: (1) simple LaunchAgent plist (recommended), (2) add to mcp-supervisor, (3) add native `nanobot service` CLI command. Drafted ai.nanobot.gateway.plist following existing pattern. User has not yet confirmed action.

Created nanobot LaunchAgent plist file at /Users/les/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.nanobot.gateway.plist (1272 bytes). Validated with plutil (OK). Status changed from "proposed, not yet created" to "created but not yet loaded". Manual nanobot process running as PID 45434 needs to be killed and launchctl load run to complete switchover. User was asked whether to proceed with load + kill or do it manually.

Brief check-in — user asked "are you there?" Assistant confirmed presence, noted the ai.nanobot.gateway.plist was created/validated and ready for launchd switchover. No new technical decisions or changes beyond what was already documented.

Confirmed nanobot LaunchAgent switchover is complete. Manual process no longer running; launchd is managing nanobot via ai.nanobot.gateway.plist (PID 47000, started 12:47AM). No manual intervention needed — switchover happened automatically. MEMORY.md updated to reflect the completed switchover status.

User asked whether launchd or autostart script manages MCP servers. Confirmed that launchd is the active manager for all 20 MCP server plists in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/. Running servers (exit code 0): mahavishnu (PID 47892), supervisor (PID 2952), session-buddy, crackerjack, akosha. Loaded but not running (exit code 78): chart-antv, excalidraw, synxis-crs/pms, mailgun, dhruva, raindropio, graphics, unifi, neo4j, n8n, porkbun-dns/domain, mermaid, grafana. Old autostart script at ~/.claude/scripts/auto-start-mcp-servers.sh is now deprecated/unused since launchd handles all lifecycle management.

Brief exchange confirming that `~/.claude/scripts/auto-start-mcp-servers.sh` is the deprecated auto-start script from before the launchd migration. No new information added — all already captured in long-term memory.

User initiated a "tidy up" session. Assistant identified cleanup candidates: (1) deprecated auto-start-mcp-servers.sh script, (2) audit of ~/.claude/scripts/ directory, (3) ~19 loaded-but-not-running LaunchAgent plists (exit code 78), (4) MEMORY.md length/consolidation. No cleanup actions taken yet — awaiting user direction on priority.

User initiated cleanup of ~/.claude/scripts/ directory (~57 scripts/files). Assistant audited contents and identified only 2 items still in use: codex-mcp-server.sh (referenced by MCP server configs) and mcp-launchd/ (launchd management). Everything else is legacy cruft: agent optimizers, old git setup scripts, health checks, qwen wrappers, session managers, hook managers, auto-start-mcp-servers.sh, etc. — all superseded by proper MCP services and launchd. User confirmed willingness to delete, said can restore from git backup. Final decision on which files to keep pending (assistant proposed keeping codex-mcp-server.sh and mcp-launchd/).

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# install-mcp-launchd.sh - One-click setup for MCP launchd services
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LAUNCHAGENTS_DIR="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents"
LOG_DIR="$HOME/.local/state/mcp/logs"
READY_DIR="$HOME/.local/state/mcp/ready"
BIN_DIR="$HOME/.local/bin"

# Create directories
create_directories() {
    log_info "Creating directories..."
    mkdir -p "$LAUNCHAGENTS_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$READY_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$BIN_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_DIR")/state"
    log_success "Directories created"
}

# Create symlink
    ln -sf "$SCRIPT_DIR/mcp-ctl.sh" "$BIN_DIR/mcp-ctl"
    chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/mcp-ctl.sh"

# Add to PATH if needed
    if ! echo "$PATH" | grep -q "$BIN_DIR"; then
        log_warn "Add $BIN_DIR to your PATH for mcp-ctl access"
    fi

log_success "mcp-ctl installed to $BIN_DIR/mcp-ctl"
}

ln -sf "$SCRIPT_DIR/mcp-supervisor.sh" "$BIN_DIR/mcp-supervisor"
    chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/mcp-supervisor.sh"

log_success "mcp-supervisor installed to $BIN_DIR/mcp-supervisor"
}
Exit code: 0

This means in-process workers work even without `MahavishnuApp` being fully initialized (e.g., in unit tests or or standalone usage). This avoids hard dependencies on initialization order.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`
